name: reslava-result-extensions
description: CodeLens and diagram preview for [ResultFlow] pipelines

enumDescriptions: Reuse a single diagram panel for all pipelines
Open a new panel for each pipeline
reslava.diagramWindowMode.markdownDescription: Controls whether clicking a pipeline opens a new panel (`multiple`) or reuses the existing one (`single`).

resultflow.tracePort.markdownDescription: Port for the ResultFlow trace HTTP endpoint. Must match the port passed to `PipelineTraceHost.Start(buffer, port)` or the Kestrel port your app is running on when using `app.MapResultFlowTraces(buffer)`.

resultflow.tracePollIntervalMs.markdownDescription: How often (in milliseconds) the Live panel polls `GET /reslava/traces`. Default: 2000 ms.

walkthroughs.title: Get started with REslava.Result Extensions
walkthroughs.description: Visualize your .NET Result pipelines in 3 steps.

steps.title: Install a ResultFlow NuGet package
steps.description:
Choose your track:

**Track A** — REslava.Result users:
```
dotnet add package REslava.Result.Flow
```
[Open terminal — Track A](command:reslava.installTrackA)

**Track B** — Any other Result library (ErrorOr, LanguageExt, FluentResults):
```
dotnet add package REslava.ResultFlow
```
[Open terminal — Track B](command:reslava.installTrackB)

Want to see a complete working example? **[REslava.Result.Flow.Demo](https://github.com/reslava/nuget-package-reslava-result/tree/main/samples/REslava.Result.Flow.Demo)** — a runnable sample covering every ResultFlow feature.
[Open Demo on GitHub](command:reslava.openDemo)

steps.title: Add [ResultFlow] to a pipeline method
steps.description:
Annotate any fluent Result method:
```csharp
[ResultFlow]
public Result<UserDto> Register(RegisterCommand cmd) =>
    CreateUser(cmd)
        .Ensure(IsValid, new ValidationError("invalid"))
        .Bind(SaveUser)
        .Map(ToDto);
```
Then **build your project** to generate the diagram constants.

Build from the terminal:
```
dotnet build
```
[Refresh Flow Catalog](command:reslava.refreshFlowCatalog)

steps.title: Open a diagram preview
steps.description:
Click **▶ Open diagram preview** above any `[ResultFlow]` method, or browse the **⚡ Flow Catalog** in the Activity Bar sidebar.

The sidebar shows every pipeline grouped by project and class — click any method to open its diagram instantly.
